clean_story: Jokes about pain.  Laughter where it stings. "Dark humor" twists taboos—death, tragedy, or fear—into punchlines, like wrapping a grenade in glitter. It’s not about the suffering. It’s the audacity to laugh at it. Example:A funeral joke—"At least he’s in a better place… his ex-wife’s house. "—works because it flips grief into surprise. The brain registers the shock first, then the absurdity. Studies show dark humor fans score higher in intelligence and emotional resilience. They’re not heartless. They’re using laughter as armor against chaos. Historical use:WWII soldiers told gallows jokes in trenches. Medics today crack morbid quips after trauma calls. It’s a pressure valve. Three related subjects are coping mechanisms, taboo breaking, and gallows humor.
